perturbation.original_text: Such proprietary or confidential information ("Information") includes any and all technical and non-technical information, including without limitation, information concerning financial, accounting or marketing reports, business plans, analysis, forecasts, predictors, intellectual property, trade secrets and know-how disclosed in connection with the Transactions.
perturbation.changed_text: Such proprietary or confidential information ("Information") includes only technical information, specifically limited to engineering data, software specifications, and patents directly relevant to the Transactions. Financial reports and business plans are explicitly excluded from the definition of 'Information' under this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition broadly covers all technical and non-technical information. The modified definition restricts it to specific types of technical information, excluding financial reports and business plans. This creates an in-text contradiction because Section 3 states that "the fact and substance of Company's discussions and correspondence with Broker relating to the Transactions" shall be deemed information, which could include financial discussions. So, this changes creates an in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information which is disclosed by one party to the other in connection with discussions relating to the Transactions, whether before or after the date of execution of this Agreement, shall automatically be deemed proprietary or confidential and subject to this Agreement unless otherwise confirmed in writing by the disclosing party. In addition, the existence and terms of this Agreement, and the fact and substance of Company's discussions and correspondence with Broker relating to the Transactions, including the identification of either party by name or identifiable in connection with the parties' participation in such process, shall be deemed information of both parties and shall not be disclosed by either party without the consent of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'Information' to exclude financial reports and business plans. The contradicted text states that all information disclosed in connection with discussions relating to the transactions, including the substance of Company's discussions, shall be deemed proprietary or confidential. Since discussions could reasonably involve financial matters, the exclusion in the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text.

perturbation.original_text: "Representatives" means the controlled affiliates of either party, and the respective directors, officers, employees, attorneys, s and other agents and advisors of either party or of the controlled affiliates of either party. Each party shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by its respective Representatives and shall take all reasonably necessary measures to restrain its Representatives from unauthorized disclosure or use of information.
perturbation.changed_text: "Representatives" means only the direct employees of either party. Attorneys, agents, and advisors are explicitly excluded from this definition. Each party shall only be responsible for breaches of this Agreement directly caused by their employees' actions and not by the actions of any other individuals or entities associated with them.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition includes a wide range of individuals and entities. The modified definition restricts it only to direct employees. This creates an in-text contradiction because, under section 10, Representatives include "employees or Representatives". If Representatives are now defined as only employees, Section 10 becomes contradictory as to who can perform the actions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that the receiving party or its employees or Representatives (a) need (for securities law purposes) to make disclosures of information or (b) are required by law, regulations, or government agency or court orders, interrogatories, requests for information or documents, subpoenas, or civil investigative demands to disclose any information, in the case of (a) the receiving party shall provide the disclosing party with prompt written notice so that the disclosing party can work with the receiving party to limit the disclosure to the greatest extent possible consistent with legal obligations (it being understood that disclosure of the name of the other party will never be made without that party's prior written consent); or in he case of (b) the receiving party shall use its reasonable efforts to minimize such disclosure and obtain an assurance that the recipient shall accord confidential treatment to the information, and shall notify the disclosing party contemporaneously of such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly narrows the definition of 'Representatives' to only direct employees, excluding attorneys, agents, and advisors. The contradicted text, Section 10, refers to the 'receiving party or its employees or Representatives'. By limiting the definition of Representatives to only employees, Section 10 now creates a contradiction because it mentions both employees and Representatives as distinct entities but now the definition of Representative is only limited to employee. This causes in-text contradiction, because if they are both the same, then Section 10 is poorly worded (at minimum) or it is contradictory as to who can perform certain actions.

perturbation.original_text: All information which is disclosed by one party to the other in connection with discussions relating to the Transactions, whether before or after the date of execution of this Agreement, shall automatically be deemed proprietary or confidential and subject to this Agreement unless otherwise confirmed in writing by the disclosing party.
perturbation.changed_text: Only information explicitly marked as 'Confidential' in writing by the disclosing party at the time of disclosure shall be deemed proprietary or confidential and subject to this Agreement. Information disclosed orally, or without such written designation, is not covered under this agreement, regardless of its nature.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states all information disclosed is automatically considered confidential unless explicitly stated otherwise. The modified text requires information to be explicitly marked as confidential in writing to be considered confidential. This creates an internal contradiction with section 5, where the receiving party has to return all "such information".
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information shall be deemed the property of the disclosing Party and, within ten (10) business days upon written request from the disclosing party, the other party will return all such information received in tangible form to the disclosing party or will destroy all such information.
perturbation.justification: The original text considers all information disclosed as confidential unless otherwise stated. The changed text flips this, requiring explicit written designation as 'Confidential' for information to be considered confidential. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states the receiving party must return 'all such information received', because the change makes it unclear what constitutes 'such information' that needs to be returned, thus creating a clear and impactful contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: With respect to information disclosed under this Agreement, the party to whom the information is disclosed and its Representatives shall: a. hold the information in confidence, exercising a degree of care not less than the care used by such party to protect its own proprietary or confidential information that it does not wish to disclose, and in no event less than a reasonable degree of care; b. restrict disclosure of the information solely to those Representatives with a need to know and not disclose it to any other person; c. advise those Representatives of their obligations with respect to the information; and d. use the information only in connection with continuing discussions by the parties concerning the Transactions, except as may otherwise be mutually agreed upon in writing, and shall reproduce such information only to the extent necessary for such purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: With respect to information disclosed under this Agreement, the party to whom the information is disclosed and its Representatives shall: a. make reasonable effort to hold the information in confidence, but will not be liable for inadvertent disclosures; b. attempt to restrict disclosure of the information solely to those Representatives with a need to know, but may disclose it to others as needed for business purposes; c. have no specific obligation to advise Representatives of their confidentiality obligations; and d. use the information primarily in connection with continuing discussions by the parties concerning the Transactions, but may also use it for other business purposes if deemed beneficial.
perturbation.explanation: This change significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations. Originally, the receiving party had to exercise a high degree of care, strictly limit disclosure, and advise Representatives of their obligations. The modified version lowers the standard to 'reasonable effort,' allows broader disclosure, removes the obligation to advise Representatives, and permits using the information for other business purposes. This contradicts with Section 16, regarding the breaching the agreement due to any breach of the provisions of this agreement
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that the disclosing party wold be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text significantly weakens the confidentiality obligations, stating that the receiving party only needs to make 'reasonable effort' and 'will not be liable for inadvertent disclosures'. It also allows for broader disclosure and removes the obligation to advise Representatives of their confidentiality obligations. The contradicted text states that the disclosing party would be irreparably injured by *any* breach of the agreement and is entitled to equitable relief in the event of *any* breach. The weakened obligations in the changed text directly contradict the idea that *any* breach would be irreparably injuring, because some breaches are now permitted due to the weakened confidentiality terms. This creates a strong in-text contradiction, as the remedy for breach is undermined.

perturbation.original_text: Information shall be deemed the property of the disclosing Party and, within ten (10) business days upon written request from the disclosing party, the other party will return all such information received in tangible form to the disclosing party or will destroy all such information.
perturbation.changed_text: Information shall be jointly owned by both parties. Upon termination of discussions, each party shall retain copies of all information and has no obligation to return or destroy anything. Each party is free to use the information as they see fit after the discussions end.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states the information remains the property of the disclosing party and must be returned or destroyed. The modified text states the information is jointly owned, with no obligation to return or destroy it, and permits either party to use it freely after discussions end. This contradicts the entire premise of the NDA, specifically section 11 which refers to intelectual property
perturbation.contradicted_text: Nothing contained in this Agreement shall be construed as granting or conferring any rights by license or otherwise in any information disclosed, or under any trademark, patent, copyright, mask work or nay other intellectual property right of either party. None of the information which may be disclosed or exchanged by the parties shall constitute any representation, warranty, assurance, guarantee or inducement by either party to the other of any kind, and, in particular, with respect to the non-infringement of trademarks, patents, copyrights, mask works or any other intellectual property right.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text are in line, stating information is the property of the disclosing party, and no rights are conferred by the agreement with respect to intellectual property. The changed text, however, explicitly states that information is jointly owned and either party can use it freely, directly contradicting the intellectual property rights restriction in the contradicted text. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because the changed text actively grants rights to use information, whereas the contradicted text explicitly denies the granting of any such rights.

perturbation.original_text: The obligations of the parties contained in Paragraphs 5, 10 and 12 shall survive and continue beyond the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period by a further period of two (2) years.
perturbation.changed_text: The obligations of the parties contained in Paragraphs 5, 10 and 12 shall cease immediately upon the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period. There are no continuing obligations beyond this point.
perturbation.explanation: This change eliminates the survival clause for specific obligations. Originally, the obligations in Paragraphs 5, 10, and 12 continued for two years after the Information Disclosure Period. The modified version states these obligations end immediately after the Information Disclosure Period. This creates a direct conflict with the original intent and would cause the disclosing party to have no legal recourse if confidential information was misused after the initial disclosure period, it also contradicts section 16 about breaching the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party agrees that the disclosing party wold be irreparably injured by a breach of this Agreement by the receiving party or its Representatives and that the disclosing party shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance, in the event of any breach of the provisions of this Agreement. Such remedies shall not be deemed to be exclusive remedies for a breach of this Agreement, but shall be addition to all other remedies available at law or in equity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that obligations in paragraphs 5, 10, and 12 cease immediately upon the expiration of the Information Disclosure Period, while the contradicted text provides for equitable relief for any breach of the agreement. Since the original text allowed obligations to survive the Information Disclosure Period, a breach after the Information Disclosure Period would have recourse, but now would not, due to the changed text. Therefore, the modified text directly contradicts the contradicted text by negating the possibility of seeking remedies for breaches related to paragraphs 5, 10, and 12 after the Information Disclosure Period, which aligns with the definition of ambiguities that leads to in-text contradiction.
